parameters.query: Software Engineer Intern in San Francisco

data.job_description:
About Mariana Minerals
Mariana Minerals is a software-first, vertically integrated minerals company on a mission to supply the critical minerals powering modern energy, AI, and defense technologies. Weâre reimagining the minerals supply chain by combining deep industry expertise with advanced software, automation, and data-driven decision-making.

The Role
We are hiring a Full Stack Software Engineer Intern to work on real, high-impact problems within our software and operations team. You will contribute directly to building internal tools and systems that support the engineering, construction, and operation of critical infrastructure.
This role is designed to provide hands-on experience shipping production-quality software. You will work closely with engineers and cross-functional stakeholders to build systems that solve real operational problems â not just prototypes or isolated features.

What Youâll Do
â¢ Own a defined project with clear deliverables by the end of the internship

â¢ Build full-stack features across frontend (React or similar) and backend services

â¢ Develop and maintain APIs, services, and data integrations

â¢ Work with internal stakeholders to understand workflows and translate them into software

â¢ Contribute to internal tools that improve efficiency of engineering and operations teams

â¢ Integrate with third-party tools and systems (APIs, data pipelines, etc.)

â¢ Collaborate with ML engineers to support data pipelines or ML-powered features where relevant

â¢ Test, debug, and iterate on production systems

â¢ Present your work, impact, and learnings at the end of the internship

Qualification
â¢ Currently pursuing a degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field

â¢ Strong programming fundamentals (Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, or similar)

â¢ Familiarity with web development (frontend frameworks and backend basics)

â¢ Hands-on experience through projects, coursework, or internships

â¢ Ability to break down problems and build end-to-end solutions

â¢ Comfortable working in ambiguous environments and learning quickly

â¢ Clear communication skills and willingness to collaborate across teams

Why Join Us?
At Mariana Minerals, youâll be part of a mission-driven team reshaping the way critical minerals are sourced and supplied globally. Youâll have the autonomy to make big decisions, the tools to innovate, and a culture that values ownership, smart automation, and collaboration.

Our culture is built on three principles:
â¢ Extreme Ownership â We take full responsibility for outcomes, relentlessly driving toward solutions.

â¢ Engineer Out Requirements, then Automate â We simplify, optimize, and then automate for scale.

â¢ Share Your Legos â We collaborate openly, share knowledge, and empower each other to build bigger, better solutions.

Join us as we build the future of responsible mineral sourcing and supply.

Mariana is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected status.

We are at the perfect time where robotics will take massive leaps, and we want to be the pioneers in how robots can come into our daily lives. Our first robot, LeLamp, explores a new category of consumer robotics where everyday objects become interactive and help us reshape our attachment to technology.

As an intern, you will be responsible for helping build the software and machine learning systems that power robot perception, intelligence, and behavior. You will work closely with the team to develop and deploy ML models, build real-time software systems, and integrate machine learning with robotic hardware â turning research and simulation into reliable, production-ready robotics software.

In this role, you will:

â¢
Develop software systems for robotic platforms that power robot perception, intelligence, and behavior

â¢
Build machine learning models for tasks such as computer vision, audio processing, and interaction understanding

â¢
Design and implement pipelines for training and deploying machine learning models

â¢
Integrate ML systems with robotic hardware and embedded systems

â¢
Improve robot perception, responsiveness, and behavioral intelligence

â¢
Collaborate with robotics engineers to build integrated robotic systems

You may be a good fit if you:

â¢
Are pursuing a degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related field

â¢
Have strong programming skills in Python and/or C++ as well as an understanding of algorithms and software engineering fundamentals.

â¢
Have experience with machine learning frameworks

â¢
Have experience in one or more of the following areas: computer vision, robotics, reinforcement learning, or multimodal AI

â¢
Have experience building and deploying machine learning systems preferably in Simulations (Issac Sim, MJLab, Mujoco, etc.)

â¢
Are comfortable jumping into unfamiliar systems and can create order through chaos.

â¢
Care about users and feel ownership over outcomes, even for systems you don't own.

â¢
Think holistically about systems and approach complex problems with creative, outside-the-box solutions.

We are looking for people who:

â¢
Are passionate about building intelligent physical systems.

â¢
Move quickly and work well in rapid iteration cycles.

â¢
Take ownership over the systems they design and build.

â¢
Are curious, resourceful, and motivated to solve difficult problems.

â¢
Work well in small, collaborative teams.

â¢
Consider not just what immediately works, but how consumers will engage with robots.

Job Details

Location: San Francisco, California

Position Type: Internship

Work Arrangement: In-person or hybrid depending on role needs

Our Culture

At Human Computer Lab, our mission is to reshape human attachment to technology. We are a fast-paced team that focuses on execution, turning all the ideas and thoughts into reality in front of us.

The early team becomes the DNA of the company. We set ourselves and others to a high standard, and we respond with kindness when things get hard but keep everyone accountable.

About Us At Hayden AI, we are on a mission to harness the power of computer vision to transform the way transit systems and other government agencies address real-world challenges. From bus lane and bus stop enforcement to transportation optimization technologies and beyond, our innovative mobile perception system empowers our clients to accelerate transit, enhance street safety, and drive toward a sustainable future. About the Engineering Operations Team The Engineering Operations Team drives quality, tooling, and deployment across our systems and development lifecycle. We work on test automation, build internal tools to support triage and debugging workflows, and assist with project execution. About the Role As an Intern at Hayden AI, you will play a handsâon role in ensuring the quality and reliability of the technology that powers our automated enforcement solutions for cities and transit agencies around the world. In this role you will contribute to test automation efforts, build internal tools to support triage and debugging workflows, and assist with project coordination across the engineering organization. You will report into the Engineering Operations Team and work closely with QA Engineers, Technical Program Managers, and Project Engineers. Weâre looking for someone who is detailâoriented, enjoys solving operational problems, and has foundational experience in software development, scripting, or testing. Curiosity and a bias toward building practical solutions are a must. This position is based in San Francisco and follows a hybrid schedule with at least 3 days inâoffice per week. Key Responsibilities Below are your primary responsibilities. These represent the core areas where youâll make an impact. As part of a rapidly evolving team, we look forward to your impact expanding over time. Develop and maintain automated tests used in validation across software and system components Build internal tools to assist with issue triage, debugging, and diagnostic workflows Support project coordination activities including tracking milestones, documenting progress, and flagging blockers Collaborate with engineers across disciplines to identify quality gaps and contribute to process improvements Required Qualifications The qualifications below outline the experience and skills most relevant to success in this role. We recognize that skills and potential come in many forms, and we welcome diverse experiences that advance our mission. Education: Currently in your final year of a Bachelorâs program, or enrolled in a Masterâs or PhD program in Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, or a related technical field. Technical Experience: Experience in one or more of the following programming languages: Python Typescript Cypress Some experience in: front end or front end testing, embedded systems validation, model evaluation Personal Attributes: Detailâoriented with a high bar for quality and accuracy. Curious and selfâdriven, motivated to dig into problems and find root causes. Strong communicator who can clearly document findings and surface issues to the right stakeholders. Collaborative team player who thrives in crossâfunctional environments. Organized and reliable, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and follow through consistently. Comfortable with ambiguity and able to make progress with limited direction. #J-18808-Ljbffr

Astranis builds advanced satellites for high orbits, expanding humanity's reach into the solar system. Today, Astranis satellites provide dedicated, secure networks to highly-sophisticated customers across the globe- large enterprises, sovereign governments, and the US military. With five satellites on orbit and many more set to launch soon, the company is servicing a backlog of more than $1 billion of commercial contracts.

Astranis is the preferred satellite communications partner for buyers with stringent requirements for uptime, data security, network visibility, and customization. Astranis has raised over $750 million from some of the world's best investors, from Andreessen Horowitz to Blackrock and Fidelity, and employs a team of 450 engineers and entrepreneurs. Astranis designs, builds, and operates its satellites out of its 153,000 sq. ft. headquarters in Northern California, USA.

Software Engineer- Backend Intern (Summer 2026)

As a Software Engineer Intern on the Platform team, you'll design and build services that autonomously control satellites, monitor telemetry for anomalies, and provide real-time situational awareness to keep our fleet safe and online. You'll also be building the core components and services that power the rest of our software organization, enabling every team to move faster and more reliably.

You will be shaping the foundation of software that has to work flawlessly - because our satellites depend on it.

This role will contribute to both our commercial and US government programs.

Internships at Astranis typically last for twelve weeks, and are hourly roles designed for students who are currently enrolled at a four-year university. If you have already graduated from a four-year university, please apply to be an Associate Engineer.
Role
â¢ Design and build high-performance, reliable, mission-critical software that is used to send commands to space
â¢ Take full ownership of features, working across backend and infrastructure
â¢ Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to define software requirements, architectures, and designs
Requirements
â¢ Currently pursuing a B.S. or M.S. in Computer Science, or equivalent degree
â¢ Strong proficiency in Python
â¢ Experience and understanding of databases (Postgres, etc)
â¢ Experience and understanding of pub/sub and streaming systems (RabbitMQ, Flink, etc)
â¢ Don't meet them all? Not a problem. Please apply even if you do not meet all these criteria.
Bonus
â¢ Experience with Kubernetes
â¢ Experience building fleet management systems

The base pay for this position is $29.00 per hour.

Base Hourly Pay

$29-$29 USD

U.S. Citizenship, Lawful Permanent Residency, or Refugee/Asylee Status Required

(To comply with U.S. Government space technology export regulations, applicant must be a U.S. citizen, lawful permanent resident of the United States, or other protected individual as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3))

Our mission and our products are meant to connect the world and everyone in it, regardless of gender, race, creed, or any other distinction. We believe in a diverse and inclusive workplace, and we encourage all people to join our team and bring their unique perspective to help make us stronger.

Netic is the AI revenue engine for essential services who are the backbone of the American economy.
With $43M in funding from Founders Fund, Greylock, Hanabi, and Dylan Field who led our Series B, we helped our customers book hundreds of thousands of jobs across services industries in North America. There are now companies operating entirely AI-first on Netic.
Youâll join our team with relentless builders from Scale, Databricks, HRT, Meta, MIT, Stanford, and Harvard in bringing frontier AI to the physical economy, where the problems are hard, the data is complex, and the impact is immediate and tangible.
At Netic, we build features that put AI in the hands of real businesses. As an intern, you'll have a dedicated mentor to guide you through the end-to-end process of supporting customers, shipping features, and iterating quickly.

12 week internship. Internships are available to start in Summer of 2026 or earlier.

What You'll Do:
â¢ Build agentic AI products: Design, code, and ship full-stack features for Netic's AI platform, within days.

â¢ Own projects end-to-end: Drive projects from spec to production, handling data models, APIs, frontâend polish, and postâlaunch iteration.

â¢ Coâcreate with customers: Work closely with our customers to understand workflows, gather feedback, and turn pain points into features that stick.

What You'll Learn:
â¢ Fullâstack fluency: Increase familiarity with React, TypeScript, Python; experience with databases and cloud infrastructure.

â¢ AI experience: You'll be handsâon with LLMs, working with state of the art models and infra.

â¢ Founderâlevel ownership: You'll see what it takes to run a successful startup, balancing scrappiness with creativity and technical excellence.

What brings us together is our commitment to:
â¢ Live to build

â¢ Run through walls and win

â¢ Obsess over customers in each line of code

â¢ Lose sleep over the "almost perfect"

â¢ Show internal locus of control

â¢ Prioritize finesse: refinement of first principles thinking, execution, and craftsmanship

We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

data.job_title: Software Engineer Intern (AI Infrastructure / Training / Inference)
data.job_description:
About The Role

We are hiring Software Engineers focused on AI Infrastructure to build the systems that enable frontier multimodal AI to operate reliably at production scale. This role exists because modern generative and vision models require infrastructure beyond traditional backend engineering â including GPU orchestration, large-scale inference systems, performance optimization, and developer platforms that allow applied scientists to move fast without sacrificing reliability or cost efficiency.

You Will Work On

â¢ Scalable model serving and inference pipelines
â¢ Distributed GPU infrastructure
â¢ Performance and cost optimization
â¢ Reliability, observability, and production readiness

You will operate at the boundary between systems engineering and machine learning â building the âpaved roadsâ that allow advanced AI systems to scale safely and efficiently.

What You'll Do

â¢ Design and build scalable infrastructure supporting training and inference workflows.
â¢ Develop high-performance APIs and backend services for AI model serving.
â¢ Optimize GPU utilization, latency, and throughput for multimodal workloads.
â¢ Build distributed systems supporting large-scale generative models.
â¢ Improve observability, monitoring, and reliability of AI systems.
â¢ Partner closely with Applied Science teams to productionize research systems.
â¢ Drive improvements in deployment workflows, automation, and platform usability.

Qualifications

â¢ Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or comparable combination of education and practical experience.
â¢ Strong object-oriented programming skills (Python, C++, Java, Go, or similar).
â¢ Strong data structures and algorithms foundations.
â¢ Experience building production backend or distributed systems.
â¢ Understanding of cloud infrastructure concepts and containerized systems.

Preferred Qualifications

â¢ Experience with Kubernetes, Docker, or container orchestration.
â¢ Familiarity with GPU-based ML workloads or distributed training/inference systems.
â¢ Experience with model serving frameworks (vLLM, Triton, Ray Serve, or similar).
â¢ Experience with observability tools and performance debugging.
â¢ Familiarity with PyTorch or ML workflows.
â¢ Interest in optimizing systems for efficiency, scalability, and developer velocity.

Why Join SPREEAI?

â¢ Real Impact & Ownership: This is an opportunity to shape a product and brand at the forefront of fashion-tech innovation. Your design work will directly impact how thousands (eventually millions) of people experience shopping with SPREEAI â no bureaucratic layers, your ideas can go live and make a difference immediately. Youâll own projects that truly matter in the companyâs growth.
â¢ Visionary Team & Exposure: Work side-by-side with a passionate founding team and collaborate with industry visionaries. Youâll have direct access to our CEO and leadership, and even get to interact with world-class advisors (our board includes an iconic fashion figure). Itâs a chance to learn from and contribute to the best of both the tech and fashion worlds.
â¢ Creative Freedom & Innovation: Join a high-growth startup environment that celebrates bold ideas and moves at lightning speed. Youâll have the autonomy to introduce new design concepts, test emerging technologies, and innovate without red tape. If youâve ever wanted to combine your love of design, luxury fashion, and cutting-edge tech, youâll have the freedom to do it here and see your vision realized.

SPREEAI is a fast-growing, innovative AI company at the forefront of fashion and e-commerce, revolutionizing how consumers engage with fashion through lifelike photorealistic try-on technology and hyper-personalized shopping experiences. Our mission is to redefine the retail landscape with cutting-edge AI solutions that blend high fashion and technology. We thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced environment where creativity meets technology to drive real impact. If you are passionate about innovation and shaping the future of fashion, SPREEAI offers a platform to make your mark.

Location

San Francisco HQ Office

Employment Type

Intern

Location Type

Hybrid

Department

Research & Development

Engineering Operations

Compensation

$45 per hour

We determine fair compensation for each position by considering market rates, qualifications, experience, and location.

This job posting reflects a salary range based on different levels within the position, which are primarily determined by location. Your interview performance will help us determine your base salary offer within this range.

Keep in mind that salary is just one piece of Hayden's total compensation package. We also offer equity and a company-wide bonus plan. Additional benefits are shared as part of this job posting.

Benefits and Perks

There are endless learning and development opportunities from a highly diverse and talented peer group, including experts in a wide range of fields (AI, Computer Vision, Government Contracting, Systems & Device Engineering, Operations, Communications, and more!)

â¢

Options for medical, dental, and vision coverage for employees and dependents (for US employees)

â¢

Flexible Spending Account (FSA) and Dependent Care Flexible Spending Account (DCFSA)

â¢

401(k) with 3% company matching

â¢

Unlimited PTO

â¢

Daily catered lunches in our San Francisco office

At Hayden AI, we are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace where everyone is treated with respect and dignity. We believe that our differences make us stronger and drive innovation. As an equal opportunity employer, we do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected status. We are dedicated to fostering a work environment that celebrates diversity and ensures that every individual has the opportunity to contribute to our mission and achieve their full potential.

Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Hayden AI employees or any other company location. Hayden AI is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

About Us

At Hayden AI, we are on a mission to harness the power of computer vision to transform the way transit systems and other government agencies address real-world challenges.

From bus lane and bus stop enforcement to transportation optimization technologies and beyond, our innovative mobile perception system empowers our clients to accelerate transit, enhance street safety, and drive toward a sustainable future.

About the Engineering Operations Team

The Engineering Operations Team drives quality, tooling, and deployment across our systems and development lifecycle. We work on test automation, build internal tools to support triage and debugging workflows, and assist with project execution.

About the Role

As an Intern at Hayden AI, you will play a hands-on role in ensuring the quality and reliability of the technology that powers our automated enforcement solutions for cities and transit agencies around the world. In this role you will contribute to test automation efforts, build internal tools to support triage and debugging workflows, and assist with project coordination across the engineering organization.

You will report into the Engineering Operations Team and work closely with QA Engineers, Technical Program Managers, and Project Engineers. We're looking for someone who is detail-oriented, enjoys solving operational problems, and has foundational experience in software development, scripting, or testing. Curiosity and a bias toward building practical solutions are a must.

This position is based in San Francisco and follows a hybrid schedule with at least 3 days in-office per week.

Key Responsibilities

Below are your primary responsibilities. These represent the core areas where youâll make an impact. As part of a rapidly evolving team, we look forward to your impact expanding over time.

â¢

Develop and maintain automated tests used in validation across software and system components

â¢

Build internal tools to assist with issue triage, debugging, and diagnostic workflows

â¢

Support project coordination activities including tracking milestones, documenting progress, and flagging blockers

â¢

Collaborate with engineers across disciplines to identify quality gaps and contribute to process improvements

Required Qualifications

The qualifications below outline the experience and skills most relevant to success in this role. We recognize that skills and potential come in many forms, and we welcome diverse experiences that advance our mission.

â¢

Education:

â¢

Currently in your final year of a Bachelorâs program, or enrolled in a Masterâs or PhD program in Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, or a related technical field.

â¢

Technical Experience:

â¢

Experience in one or more of the following programming languages:

â¢

Python

â¢

Typescript

â¢

Cypress

â¢

Some experience in: front end or front end testing, embedded systems validation, model evaluation

â¢

Personal Attributes:

â¢

Detail-oriented with a high bar for quality and accuracy.

â¢

Curious and self-driven, motivated to dig into problems and find root causes.

â¢

Strong communicator who can clearly document findings and surface issues to the right stakeholders.

â¢

Collaborative team player who thrives in cross-functional environments.

â¢

Organized and reliable, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and follow through consistently.

â¢

Comfortable with ambiguity and able to make progress with limited direction.

data.job_description:
Location

Strava SF

DepartmentInternships

â¢
$40 per hour â¢ Includes housing stipend

At Strava, we know our employees are the most important ingredient to our success, and our compensation and total rewards programs reflect that. We take a market-based approach to pay, and pay may vary depending on the department and your location. Salary ranges are categorized into one of three zones based on a cost of labor index for that geographic area. We will determine the candidateâs starting pay based on job-related skills, experience, qualifications, work location, and market conditions. We may modify these ranges in the future.

For more information, please contact your talent partner.

About Strava

Strava is the app for active people. With over 180 million athletes in more than 185 countries, itâs more than tracking workoutsâitâs where people make progress together, from new habits to new personal bests. No matter your sport or how you track it, Stravaâs got you covered. Find your crew, crush your goals, and make every effort count. Start your journey with Strava today.

Our mission is simple: to motivate people to live their best active lives. We believe in the power of movement to connect and drive people forward.

As an Engineering Intern at Strava, you will be embedded in a team working on real projects that ship to our athletes and within the company, treated like a full-time member of the engineering team. You'll collaborate with cross functional teams that help us support and deliver to our athletes faster, while keeping them inspired and motivated.

The Trust & Safety team is responsible for building the features, tools, systems, and infrastructure that protect the integrity of Stravaâs platform and support a safe, trusted experience for athletes worldwide. The team focuses on developing scalable and resilient solutions that detect abuse, prevent misuse, and uphold community standards.

Weâll give you experienced mentors and all the tools you need to make a meaningful impact on our product. To see examples of what past interns have contributed, read our Engineering Blog!

Available Internship:

Strava is looking for software engineering interns for a range of disciplines. This mobile Android internship would be on the Trust & Safety team. The Trust & Safety team is responsible for building the features, tools, systems, and infrastructure that protect the integrity of Stravaâs platform and support a safe, trusted experience for athletes worldwide. The team focuses on developing scalable and resilient solutions that detect abuse, prevent misuse, and uphold community standards.

We follow a hybrid model that translates to more than half of your time on-site in our San Francisco office â four days per week.

What Youâll Do:
â¢
Build and improve safety-related mobile features and internal tools alongside a collaborative team of talented engineers.

â¢
Help strengthen a safe, trusted community experience for millions of athletes around the world.

â¢
Partner closely with teams across product, design, marketing, analytics, and support to deliver impactful solutions.

â¢
Write clean, reliable, well-tested code that is easy to read, maintain, and scale.

What Youâll Bring to the Team:
â¢
Are currently pursuing a Bachelorâs degree in Computer Science or a related field, with an expected graduation date of May 2026, December 2026, or May 2027 â ORâ have recently completed or are currently enrolled in a coding bootcamp, technical training program, or are transitioning into software engineering through equivalent practical experience.

â¢

Have experience with at least one programming language such as Kotlin, Java, Python, or similar (Kotlin or Android experience is a plus).

â¢
Are passionate about building high-quality mobile experiences and improving apps used by real people.

If you donât meet every single qualification but are excited about the role, we encourage you to apply.

The Summer Internship Program:
â¢
The program will be in our San Francisco, CA office with a hybrid office attendance requirement of 4 days per week

â¢
Housing Stipend for Summer is provided to live in SF for 3 months

â¢ Length of program: 10-12 weeks (Summer 2026 roughly June through August).

â¢ Working Hours: 40 hours per week

â¢ This is a paid internship.

Why Join Us?

Movement brings us together. At Strava, weâre building the worldâs largest community of active people, helping them stay motivated and achieve their goals.

Our global team is passionate about making movement fun, meaningful, and accessible to everyone. Whether youâre shaping the technology, growing our community, or driving innovation, your work at Strava makes an impact.

When you join Strava, youâre not just joining a companyâyouâre joining a movement. If youâre ready to bring your energy, ideas, and drive, letâs build something incredible together.

Strava builds software that makes the best part of our athletesâ days even better. Just as weâre deeply committed to unlocking their potential, weâre dedicated to providing a world-class, inclusive workplace where our employees can grow and thrive, too. Weâre backed by Sequoia Capital, TCV, Madrone Partners and Jackson Square Ventures, and weâre expanding in order to exceed the needs of our growing community of global athletes. Our culture reflects our community. We are continuously striving to hire and engage teammates from all backgrounds, experiences and perspectives because we know we are a stronger team together.

Strava is an equal opportunity employer. In keeping with the values of Strava, we make all employment decisions including hiring, evaluation, termination, promotional and training opportunities, without regard to race, religion, color, sex, age, national origin, ancestry, sexual orientation, physical handicap, mental disability, medical condition, disability, gender or identity or expression, pregnancy or pregnancy-related condition, marital status, height and/or weight.

We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.
